---
title: "Publications"
---

::: {.section}
::: {.section-title}
## Publications by field

This page lists verified publications from the University of Newcastle profile and selected indexed sources. Additions can be made by editing this page or by replacing `assets/publications.bib` with a Google Scholar BibTeX export.
:::

## Health preference methods and choice modelling

<div class="pub"><span class="field">DCE methods</span><div class="pub-title">Guidance or Misdirection? Unpacking the Role of Feedback in Health Preference Assessments</div><div class="pub-meta">Genie MG, Reed SD, Ozdemir S · <em>Health Economics</em> · 2026 · DOI: 10.1002/hec.70093</div></div>
<div class="pub"><span class="field">DCE methods</span><div class="pub-title">Fit for purpose? Assessing the robustness of discrete choice experiment designs in the context of evolving health technologies</div><div class="pub-meta">Genie MG, Watson V, Schwarzinger M, Luchini S · <em>Social Science & Medicine</em> · 2026 · DOI: 10.1016/j.socscimed.2026.119463</div></div>
<div class="pub"><span class="field">DCE methods</span><div class="pub-title">Choice Consistency in Discrete Choice Experiments: Does Numeracy Skill Matter?</div><div class="pub-meta">Genie MG, Poudel N, Paolucci F, Ngorsuraches S · <em>Value in Health</em> · 2024 · DOI: 10.1016/j.jval.2024.07.001</div></div>
<div class="pub"><span class="field">Eye tracking</span><div class="pub-title">Keeping an eye on cost: What can eye tracking tell us about attention to cost information in discrete choice experiments?</div><div class="pub-meta">Genie MG, Ryan M, Krucien N · <em>Health Economics</em> · 2023 · DOI: 10.1002/hec.4658</div></div>
<div class="pub"><span class="field">DCE methods</span><div class="pub-title">To pay or not to pay? Cost information processing in the valuation of publicly funded healthcare</div><div class="pub-meta">Genie MG, Ryan M, Krucien N · <em>Social Science & Medicine</em> · 2021 · DOI: 10.1016/j.socscimed.2021.113822</div></div>
<div class="pub"><span class="field">DCE methods</span><div class="pub-title">Weighting or aggregating? Investigating information processing in multi-attribute choices</div><div class="pub-meta">Genie MG, Krucien N, Ryan M · <em>Health Economics</em> · 2021 · DOI: 10.1002/hec.4245</div></div>
<div class="pub"><span class="field">Transplant preferences</span><div class="pub-title">The role of heterogeneity of patients’ preferences in kidney transplantation</div><div class="pub-meta">Genie MG, Nicolo A, Pasini G · <em>Journal of Health Economics</em> · 2020 · DOI: 10.1016/j.jhealeco.2020.102331</div></div>

## Vaccine policy, mandates and pandemic response

<div class="pub"><span class="field">Mandates</span><div class="pub-title">No Jab, No Access? Preferences for Vaccine Mandates in Europe and Australia</div><div class="pub-meta">Genie M et al. · <em>Australian and New Zealand Journal of Public Health</em> · 2026 · DOI: 10.1016/j.anzjph.2026.100450</div></div>
<div class="pub"><span class="field">Mandates</span><div class="pub-title">Community First or My Body First? Moral Values and Vaccine Mandate Preferences</div><div class="pub-meta">Genie M et al. · <em>Australian and New Zealand Journal of Public Health</em> · 2026 · DOI: 10.1016/j.anzjph.2026.100496</div></div>
<div class="pub"><span class="field">Mandates</span><div class="pub-title">An open repository of COVID-19 vaccine mandate studies with a worked scoping review of quasi-experimental evidence</div><div class="pub-meta">Martinenghi FI et al. · <em>npj Vaccines</em> · 2026 · DOI: 10.1038/s41541-026-01454-4</div></div>
<div class="pub"><span class="field">Vaccine uptake</span><div class="pub-title">Vaccine uptake in the context of mandate announcement and removal: Evidence from Europe and North America</div><div class="pub-meta">Gebremariam AG, Genie M et al. · <em>Vaccine</em> · 2026 · DOI: 10.1016/j.vaccine.2026.128825</div></div>
<div class="pub"><span class="field">Vaccine preferences</span><div class="pub-title">Time preferences and COVID-19 vaccination uptake</div><div class="pub-meta">Attema AE, Antonini M, Genie M, Torbica A, Paolucci F · <em>European Journal of Health Economics</em> · 2026 · DOI: 10.1007/s10198-025-01801-7</div></div>
<div class="pub"><span class="field">Vaccine policy</span><div class="pub-title">Are we ready for the next pandemic? Public preferences and trade-offs between vaccine characteristics and societal restrictions across 21 countries</div><div class="pub-meta">Antonini M, Genie MG et al. · <em>Social Science & Medicine</em> · 2025 · DOI: 10.1016/j.socscimed.2025.117687</div></div>
<div class="pub"><span class="field">Vaccine attitudes</span><div class="pub-title">Democracy, Trust, and Political Orientation: Disentangling Mechanisms Shaping Individuals’ Vaccine Attitudes</div><div class="pub-meta">Genie M · <em>Journal of Health Politics, Policy and Law</em> · 2025 · DOI: 10.1215/03616878-12166725</div></div>
<div class="pub"><span class="field">Pandemic policy</span><div class="pub-title">Public acceptability of non-pharmaceutical interventions to control a pandemic in the UK: a discrete choice experiment</div><div class="pub-meta">Loria-Rebolledo LE, Ryan M, Watson V, Genie MG et al. · <em>BMJ Open</em> · 2022 · DOI: 10.1136/bmjopen-2021-054155</div></div>
<div class="pub"><span class="field">Protocol</span><div class="pub-title">Understanding public preferences and trade-offs for government responses during a pandemic: a protocol for a discrete choice experiment in the UK</div><div class="pub-meta">Genie MG et al. · <em>BMJ Open</em> · 2020 · DOI: 10.1136/bmjopen-2020-043477</div></div>

## Applied health economics and clinical preferences

<div class="pub"><span class="field">Equity</span><div class="pub-title">Priority for Self or Others? Incorporating Equity Considerations in a Preference-Based Health Value Assessment</div><div class="pub-meta">Genie MG, Ngorsuraches S · <em>Value in Health</em> · 2026 · DOI: 10.1016/j.jval.2025.10.011</div></div>
<div class="pub"><span class="field">Loneliness</span><div class="pub-title">Feeling lonely? Preferences for support programmes to reduce loneliness among older adults in Australia: A discrete choice experiment</div><div class="pub-meta">Genie MG, Boyes AW, Paolucci F · <em>Health Policy</em> · 2026 · DOI: 10.1016/j.healthpol.2026.105587</div></div>
<div class="pub"><span class="field">Migration</span><div class="pub-title">Immigration and preventive care utilization in Europe: evidence from retrospective data</div><div class="pub-meta">Buia RE, Genie M, Orso CE, Pasini G · <em>Empirical Economics</em> · 2026 · DOI: 10.1007/s00181-026-02928-z</div></div>
<div class="pub"><span class="field">Diabetes</span><div class="pub-title">Important Continuous Glucose Monitoring Attributes for Patients With Type 2 Diabetes</div><div class="pub-meta">Lai TC, Whitley HP, Genie M, Eckel RH, Ngorsuraches S · <em>Clinical Diabetes</em> · 2025 · DOI: 10.2337/cd25-0047</div></div>
<div class="pub"><span class="field">Cancer preferences</span><div class="pub-title">Patients' Preferences for Cytoreductive Treatments in Newly Diagnosed Metastatic Prostate Cancer: The IP5-MATTER Study</div><div class="pub-meta">Connor MJ, Genie M et al. · <em>European Urology Oncology</em> · 2025 · DOI: 10.1016/j.euo.2024.06.010</div></div>
<div class="pub"><span class="field">Cancer preferences</span><div class="pub-title">A Systematic Review of Patients' Values, Preferences, and Expectations for the Treatment of Metastatic Prostate Cancer</div><div class="pub-meta">Connor MJ, Genie MG et al. · <em>European Urology Open Science</em> · 2022 · DOI: 10.1016/j.euros.2021.10.003</div></div>

## Health policy and technology

<div class="pub"><span class="field">Policy</span><div class="pub-title">Telehealth evolution and policy response in Australia: Insights from the COVID-19 pandemic</div><div class="pub-meta">Bruzzo-Gallardo S, Genie M, Gallagher R, Paolucci F · Book chapter · 2025 · DOI: 10.1016/B978-0-443-30168-1.00002-5</div></div>
<div class="pub"><span class="field">Policy</span><div class="pub-title">Political determinants of COVID-19 restrictions and vaccine rollouts: The case of regional elections in Italy and Spain</div><div class="pub-meta">Prieto PA, Antonini M, Ammi M, Genie M, Paolucci F · <em>Health Policy</em> · 2024 · DOI: 10.1016/j.healthpol.2024.105082</div></div>
<div class="pub"><span class="field">Policy</span><div class="pub-title">Public preferences for vaccination campaigns in the COVID-19 endemic phase: insights from the VaxPref database</div><div class="pub-meta">Antonini M, Genie MG et al. · <em>Health Policy and Technology</em> · 2024 · DOI: 10.1016/j.hlpt.2024.100849</div></div>
<div class="pub"><span class="field">Policy</span><div class="pub-title">The COVID-19 pandemic in Italy: Policy and technology impact on health and non-health outcomes</div><div class="pub-meta">Berardi C, Antonini M, Genie MG et al. · <em>Health Policy and Technology</em> · 2020 · DOI: 10.1016/j.hlpt.2020.08.019</div></div>

::: {.callout}
The University of Newcastle profile lists 41 journal articles, 2 chapters, 10 conference outputs, 4 preprints, and 1 dataset. This page includes the main verified entries available from that profile and indexed records. To make the page exhaustive, export all Google Scholar entries as BibTeX and replace `assets/publications.bib`.
:::
